Named after a local union leader, this narrow, rather unadorned square connects downtown San Francisco to the historic ferry terminal. At the center is a statue of a naked woman, about 15 meters high, which currently clearly characterizes the square.

Although there are some benches and trees, the place does not invite you to a longer stay. It is too hectic, especially because of the street noise and the many passers-by.

This is a very nice spot on the Embarcadero.  A long walk on the Embarcadero has become one of the best things you can do in the City.  You will see some great sites, the Bay and bridges.  And this Plaza is dedicated to an important labor leader, an organizer for the laborers at the old port.  Important city history.
